color-palette--docs.name: Docs
color-palette--docs.title: Color Palette
color-palette--docs.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import ColorBox from './ColorBox'; # Color Palette Here are listed colors used. Refer only to colox box background colors. Feel free to override colors as per [Tailwind CSS theme set up](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/theme) ## 🎨 Semantic Color System (Light & Dark Mode) This system is built around the role of the color, not its literal value (e.g., color-primary instead of color-blue-600).

Light

Dark

Brand color

Usually remains the same (unless the brand must be subtle in dark mode).

Branding: Used for the logo and non-interactive brand elements.

Primary

Primary action buttons, active links, main icons.

Action/Focus: The main focal point of the interface.

Secondary

Secondary action buttons, supporting navigation elements.

Support/Alternative: Lower-priority actions.

tertiary

Divider lines, decorative icons, subtle hover states.

Decoration/Subtlety: Background or structural elements.

Light (Tonal)

Low-emphasis buttons (e.g., "Cancel"), background highlights for active menu items, or ghost-style components.

Tonal/Low Emphasis: Used for tertiary actions that need to remain visible but should not distract from the Primary or Secondary focal points.

## 📝 Text and Surface Colors (Dynamic Theme) These colors are critical for readability and must switch between light and dark modes.

Surface base

Page or main section background.

Canvas: The broadest background color.

Surface panel

Background for cards, modals, side panels.

Elevation: Should be slightly darker than surface-base in light mode, and slightly lighter in dark mode to create a "floating" effect.

Text primary

Main body text, H1/H2 headings.

Readability: The darkest/lightest text color.

Text secondary

Captions, helper text, form field placeholders.

Details/Subtlety: Lower contrast than primary text.

Text on primary

Text or icon placed on a primary background (color-primary)

Contrast: Must ensure AA or AAA contrast ratio with color-primary. Often white or black.

## 🚦 State Colors (Feedback and Validation) These colors indicate status and should have dark mode equivalents to ensure they remain legible on dark backgrounds.

Success

Successful notifications, OK status indicators.

Green (Good).

Warning

Non-blocking alerts, unstable status indicators.

Yellow/Orange (Caution).

Danger

Error messages, deletion, critical status indicators.

Red (Stop/Error).

Info

Neutral informative messages, information banners.

Light Blue/Cyan (Information).

Focus

Focus rings for accessibility (a11y), active field outlines.

Typically a brand color with high opacity.

navigation-navbar--docs.name: Docs
navigation-navbar--docs.title: Navigation/Navbar
navigation-navbar--docs.content:
import { Meta, Story, ArgTypes } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import { Navbar as NavbarEl } from '../../lib/Navigation/Navbar';
import * as NavbarStories from './Navbar.stories';

<Meta title="Navigation/Navbar" component={NavbarEl} />

# Navbar

A responsive navigation bar component for React applications, supporting branding, navigation links, and custom actions. Designed for use with React Router.

## Usage

```tsx
import { Navbar } from '@soilhat/react-components/Navigation';

<Navbar
  logoURl="https://soilhat.github.io/logo192.png"
  brandName="BrandName"
  links={[
    { label: 'Home', href: '/' },
    { label: 'About', href: '/about' },
    { label: 'Contact', href: '/contact' },
  ]}
  actions={<button className="px-4 py-2 bg-accent text-white rounded">Action</button>}
/>;
```

## Props

<ArgTypes of={NavbarEl} />

## Example

<Story of={NavbarStories.Navbar} />

## Accessibility

- Uses semantic HTML elements (`<header>`, `<nav>`, `<ul>`, `<li>`)
- All interactive elements have accessible labels
- Mobile menu button uses `aria-label` and `aria-expanded`
- Keyboard and screen reader friendly

## Best Practices

- Use with React Router for navigation (`NavLink` is used internally)
- Provide descriptive `brandName` and `logoURl` for branding
- Pass custom actions as React elements for flexibility
